rulings.Case Title: JAMES KEARNEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant American Airlines, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. The sole claim against defendant is as a ¿premises owner defendant¿ who is alleged to be liable as a ¿premises owner.¿ Pleadings frame the issues for summary judgment. Defendant satisfied its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant is liable as a premises owner under any of the premises liability theories, which are a subset of negligence theories. Plaintiffs have not presented any evidence creating a triable issue that defendant is liable as a premises owner under any of the premises liability theories. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NATALIE BUFORD et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Court Approval Of Settlement As Being In Good Faith, And For Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Intervenors HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, WEST AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY, SAFECO INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA, TRAVELERS INDEMNITY CO., SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO ZURICH INSURANCE CO., US BRANCH (as liability insurance carriers to cross-defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.)'s motion for court approval of settlement with plaintiff as being in good faith and for dismissal is granted. Rich-Tex¿s suspended status does not preclude intervenors from making this motion. Intervenors satisfied their burden of making a sufficient showing of all the pertinent Tech-Bilt factors. Cross-complainant Marc Paul did not satisfy its burden that the 25% of the settlement amount paid for Rich Tex¿s liability attributable to the wrongful death claim was either too high or too low. Nor did cross-complainant satisfy its burden that $10,625, viewed from the perspective of the time that the release was signed by Mr. Buford, was outside the Tech-Bilt ballpark, especially given Mr. Buford¿s diagnosis of lung cancer and his smoking history. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LLOYD KENDRICK et al VS. BORGWARNER, INC. INDIVIDUALLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Motor Parts Distributors, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of punitive damages is off calendar at the request of the moving party. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH FERNANDEZ VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Honeywell International Inc., F/K/A Alliedsignal, Successor-in-Interest to the Bendix Corporation's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ request for punitive damages is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. Assuming without deciding that Mr. Martin¿s letter of September 12, 1966 is admissible, and notwithstanding its obvious offensiveness to a 2011 reader, nothing in that letter shows that defendant¿s predecessor knew of the safety hazards of its asbestos-containing products. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT NELSON et al VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Georgia-Pacific LLC¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Nelson was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. By failing to submit plaintiffs¿ responses to its special discovery with its moving papers, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. The court declines to consider the evidence submitted with defendant¿s reply papers. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BILLY S. JOHNSON VS. ADVOCATE MINES, LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Cost Sought By Defendant Carlisle Corporation
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Plaintiff¿s motion to tax costs sought by defendant Carlisle Corporation is granted in part and denied in part. Of the $1570.20 sought in the costs memo for filing and motion fees, $112.50 is taxed because it was not included in the costs worksheet and another $70 is taxed (included in the Lexis-Nexis filing fees) because there is insufficient description for what those costs were for and thus the court cannot determine whether those costs were reasonably necessary. Of the $13,653.32 stated in the costs worksheet for deposition costs, the following items are taxed because there is no explanation in defendant¿s papers who the deponents are and thus the court cannot determine whether the costs for those depositions were reasonably necessary: $545 for the deposition of Michael Ellenbecker, $965.95 for the deposition of Manuel Joaquin, $831,50 for the deposition of Mark Taylor, $60.19 for the deposition of Lawrence Bean and $160.75 for the deposition of David Weill. The entirely of the $182.75 sought in the costs memo for models, blowups and photocopies is taxed because no supporting documentation or information was provided for those costs. All other costs sought in the costs memo are allowed because they are permitted under the costs statutes and were reasonably incurred. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR PAILLET VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Lehigh Hanson, Inc.¿s motion to quash service of summons of first amended complaint is continued to August 11, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220 at the request of the parties. = (220/HEK)
